methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Lumina Vehicles is a seed-stage company operating in Robotics & Manufacturing, founded by Ahmed Shubber and Federico Di Palma. The comparable universe consists of 9 peers: 1 public company (ticker 277810.KQ) and 8 private firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The Robotics & Manufacturing sector contains 11 same-sector peers overall, but screening by stage, adjacent stage, or valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints in any single filtered category. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce substantial methodological noise given the heterogeneity of robotics subsectors and business models.

As a result, this analysis cannot proceed via conventional triangulation. The lack of sufficient seed-stage or near-stage comparables in the robotics manufacturing space—a sector characterized by long development cycles, capital intensity, and technology differentiation—limits the applicability of valuation multiples derived from either the private or public peer set. A meaningful peer-median approach requires at least three comparable transactions or valuations within a narrow band; this threshold is not met.

The key uncertainty is whether public market multiples from 277810.KQ or later-stage private valuations from the identified peers provide relevant anchors for a seed-stage robotics company. Without meeting the three-company minimum, further valuation work should rely on alternative methods such as venture capital method analysis or detailed bottom-up unit economics modeling to establish a defensible range.
